name: Buddy CI/CD API
what_it_does: CI/CD platform REST API for managing pipelines, triggering builds, managing deployments, and querying execution history — with visual pipeline builder, Docker-native execution, and integrations with 100+ services.
use_cases: Triggering CI/CD pipelines programmatically from external agents or webhooks
Querying pipeline execution status and logs for build monitoring agents
Managing deployment environments and access permissions via API
Creating and updating pipeline configurations as code via REST API
Integrating build status into external dashboards and ChatOps workflows
not_for: Complex multi-cloud deployments (use Octopus Deploy or Harness)
Kubernetes GitOps workflows (use ArgoCD or Flux)
Massive enterprise CI at high scale (Jenkins or GitHub Actions for scale)
best_when: A small to mid-size team needs an easy-to-use CI/CD platform with visual pipeline builder and API access for automation without Jenkins complexity.
avoid_when: You need massive scale CI or complex enterprise deployment orchestration.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for third-party application access. Personal access tokens for direct API access. Scopes: workspace, project, pipeline, repository access. Token per workspace.

pricing.notes: Generous free tier for small teams. Build-minute based pricing for on-demand. Competitive with CircleCI and Semaphore. Visual pipeline builder reduces config complexity.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documented with examples. Pipeline execution is async — agents must poll for completion. Webhook delivery for build events. Error responses include codes and messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Pipeline trigger creates new execution each time — not idempotent. Read operations are idempotent. Pipeline configuration updates are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit of 100 requests/minute can be hit quickly by polling agents
Pipeline execution is async — poll execution ID for status
Visual pipeline builder in UI doesn't map directly to API pipeline spec format
Workspace slug required for all API requests — not just project ID

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2, GDPR compliant. OAuth2 for access. TLS enforced. Scoped tokens per workspace. EU data residency option. Pipeline secret variable management available.
